message: bd-iuvb.17.7: PTY inline mouse capture defaults

@@ -1180,16 +1180,16 @@
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.3","title":"Action Timeline: click-to-select events + wheel scroll + filter shortcuts","description":"Background
- ActionTimeline is an event stream viewer.

Goal
- Add mouse selection, wheel scrolling, and shortcuts for filtering and jumping.

Mouse Interactions
- Click event row selects and shows details.
- Wheel scrolls list.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `J/K`: next/prev event.
- `F`: filter.
- `G`: jump to latest.

Acceptance Criteria
- Selection is deterministic; no flicker when events append.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/action_timeline.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","timeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:03.949610455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.4","title":"Virtualized Search: mouse row selection + wheel scroll + search shortcuts","description":"Background
- VirtualizedSearch combines list virtualization with fuzzy search.

Goal
- Mouse selection + wheel scroll should be perfect; add shortcuts for search modes.

Mouse Interactions
- Click selects row.
- Wheel scrolls.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `/`: focus search.
- `Enter`: open selected.

Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior consistent with other list screens.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.5","title":"Async Tasks: click start/cancel + progress interactions + shortcuts","description":"Background
- AsyncTasks demonstrates job lifecycle and progress.

Goal
- Make task operations clickable and add shortcuts for spawning/cancelling.

Mouse Interactions
- Click to start new task.
- Click cancel on a row.
- Wheel scrolls task list.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `N`: new task.
- `C`: cancel selected.
- `R`: reset.

Acceptance Criteria
- No nondeterministic behavior introduced by timing; use deterministic tick/time where needed.

- Click/toggle stress knobs.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","title":"Explainability Cockpit: mouse-first evidence navigation + shortcut toggles","description":"Background
- ExplainabilityCockpit is a flagship 'trust' screen.

Goal
- Mouse navigation through evidence panes should be natural; add shortcuts to toggle ledgers and overlays.

Mouse Interactions
- Click tabs/sections within the cockpit.
- Wheel scrolls evidence text.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `E`: toggle evidence overlay.
- `N/P`: next/prev evidence entry.

Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence navigation is deterministic and snapshot-friendly.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","title":"Demo Input: central mouse dispatcher (hit grid + semantic events)","description":"Background
- The demo already enables hit testing (`Frame::enable_hit_testing`) and caches a hit grid for tab-bar navigation.
- Today, only the tab bar click path is routed (`handle_mouse_tab_click`). Many screens already register hit regions (panes, links, etc.) but they are not wired to input.

Goal
- Build a central, deterministic mouse dispatch layer in the demo app that routes mouse events to the right target:
  - Chrome (tabs, category tabs, status bar toggles).
  - Overlays (command palette, help overlay, tour overlay, modal stacks).
  - Active screen content.

Scope / Work Items
- Standardize the input pipeline:
  - Raw `Event::Mouse(MouseEvent)` -> (optional) semantic gestures -> dispatch.
  - Decide click activation edge: prefer `MouseUp` for buttons (prevents accidental drags) unless explicitly needed.
- Hit test on every relevant mouse event:
  - Use `(HitId, HitRegion, HitData)` from the cached hit grid.
  - Provide a consistent mapping from hit targets to actions.
- Add hover tracking:
  - Mouse move updates a `hover_hit` state (coalesced) to support hover highlight + status hints.
- Drag handling:
  - Minimal drag state machine for screens needing drag (kanban, drag-drop, sliders, timeline scrub).
  - Use existing `ftui-core` semantic event primitives if/when helpful.

Determinism / Performance Considerations
- Coalesce mouse move events aggressively (existing event coalescer already helps).
- Avoid per-frame allocations for routing; keep hit ids stable.
- Never introduce wall-clock time dependencies in gesture recognition unless time-freeze is part of determinism mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any registered hit region produces exactly one deterministic action.
- Hover state updates do not cause flicker and are coalesced.
- Drag interactions have a well-formed lifecycle and can be cancelled.
- Mouse interactions stop the guided tour consistently (like tab clicks already do).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for routing priority (palette > help > chrome > screen) and for hit mapping.
- Deterministic integration test: simulate a sequence of mouse events and assert resulting state transitions.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid cache + current mouse handling)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (click/drag semantics)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid API)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperCastle","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:25:03.083030865Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:25:03.083010357Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","demo-showcase","hit-testing","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.260566256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":836,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedSnow claiming this bead. Will implement central mouse dispatcher with: (1) MouseDispatcher struct for routing priority (palette > help > tour > chrome > screen), (2) hover tracking with coalescing, (3) drag state machine, (4) click activation on MouseUp, (5) deterministic logging. Building on existing HitGrid infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:58:47Z"},{"id":838,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedSnow: All implementation committed + 19 unit tests passing. Tests cover: drag lifecycle, click activation (Down+Up), palette dismissal priority, hover coalescing, overlay/status click handlers. Full quality gates pass (1446 lib tests, clippy, fmt). Ready for review.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:24:50Z"},{"id":839,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon: Verified MouseDispatcher implementation meets all acceptance criteria: (1) deterministic click routing via priority chain (palette>overlay>status>tab>pane), (2) hover coalescing via update_hover(), (3) drag state machine (Idle->PendingDrag->Dragging) with cancel_drag(), (4) tour stops on mouse_scroll/mouse_tab/mouse_overlay. 20 tests covering state transitions, drag threshold, hover coalescing, palette dismiss, status toggles. All 1427 lib tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:24:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","title":"Widgets: standard hit regions + mouse semantics for core widgets","description":"Background
- The render layer supports hit testing via a per-cell hit grid.
- Many widgets already accept `hit_id(...)` and register hit regions in frames, but mouse semantics are not uniformly defined or exploited in the demo.

Goal
- Define and implement consistent mouse semantics for the core widget primitives used throughout the demo:
  - Lists, tables, trees, scrollbars, buttons/toggles, modals.

Scope / Work Items
- Hit region conventions:
  - Row/entry hit data encodes index (stable) so click can select/activate the right item.
  - Scrollbar hit regions include track/thumb and provide proportional scroll-to behavior.
  - Buttons/toggles use `HitRegion::Button` and activate on mouse up.
- Interaction semantics:
  - Click selects.
  - Double click activates/opens (where meaningful).
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the widget under hover/focus.
  - Drag thumb scroll works on scrollbars.
- Accessibility:
  - Hover and focus states are visible with high-contrast + large-text modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- The same mouse gestures behave the same across all screens using these widgets.
- Hit IDs and hit data are stable and deterministic across frames.

Tests / Validation
- Extend `ftui-widgets` frame integration tests for hit registration and hit data correctness.
- Add snapshot tests showing hover/selected states for at least one list and one table.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid)
- `crates/ftui-widgets/*` (widgets)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderHollow","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:09:06.982001849Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:09:06.981974988Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui-widgets","hit-testing","mouse","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":818,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FuchsiaHill claiming this bead. Will implement consistent hit regions + mouse semantics for core widgets (lists, tables, trees, scrollbars, buttons/toggles, modals).","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:02:16Z"},{"id":842,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"IndigoLake","text":"I’m implementing List hover API (List::hover_style + ListState.hovered + moved-hover semantics) to fix current clippy break from ftui-harness widget_snapshots expecting those APIs. Will report once clippy/tests are green.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:43:30Z"},{"id":845,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon contributed 10 integration tests for widget hit regions (49a9e9ad): scrollbar track_pos + part encoding, table/list row index, hit_id opt-in, multi-widget coexistence, render determinism. Fixed scrollbar test to match actual 2-field encoding.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:12:45Z"},{"id":847,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 3 dialog hit region integration tests (commit 2be1f7d0): dialog_modal_backdrop_covers_full_area, dialog_without_hit_id_has_no_hit_regions, dialog_closed_state_renders_nothing. Total hit region tests now: 13 (10 scrollbar/list/table + 3 dialog). NOTE: Found modal container rendering-order bug in container.rs lines 409-415 — backdrop/content hit regions are registered AFTER content renders, overwriting button hit regions registered by Dialog. This means button-level hit testing is broken for any widget inside a Modal. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:29:05Z"},{"id":849,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 2 snapshot tests (commit 86b1f48a): list_selection_styled_ansi (ANSI with bold highlight style) and list_with_hit_id_and_selection (verifies both visual output and hit region data encoding). Snapshot files: list_selection_styled.ansi.snap, list_hit_id_selected.snap. This addresses the acceptance criteria 'Add snapshot tests showing hover/selected states for at least one list and one table' — list selection snapshots now have both plain text and ANSI variants; table selected+hover ANSI snapshot already existed. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:37:57Z"},{"id":851,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed the modal container rendering-order bug (commit b34655d7): Modal now registers backdrop/content hit regions BEFORE inner widget renders, so dialog button hit regions are preserved. Updated dialog_modal_backdrop_covers_full_area test to verify all three hit region layers (backdrop Custom(1), content Custom(2), button Custom(10)) are present. This was the root cause of broken button-level hit testing for all widgets inside Modals. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","title":"Demo Chrome: tabs/palette/help/status overlays fully mouse interactive","description":"Background
- The demo chrome renders the tab bar, status bar, help overlay, and command palette.
- Tab bar hit regions exist and tab switching works, but most other chrome is keyboard-only.

Goal
- Make the demo's chrome and overlays fully mouse-clickable and scrollable in the obvious way.

Scope / Work Items
- Tab bar:
  - Hover highlight, click-to-switch (already exists), and optional scroll-wheel-to-cycle.
- Category tabs (if rendered):
  - Click category pill to jump.
- Command palette:
  - Hover highlight; click selects; double click executes; wheel scrolls list.
- Help overlay:
  - Clickable close button; wheel scroll if content overflows.
- Status bar:
  - Clickable affordances for the most common toggles (help, palette, a11y, perf HUD, debug) without ambiguity.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can navigate the entire demo UI with mouse alone (except text input entry).
- Mouse interactions are logged when demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for hit-id mapping for chrome elements.
- Snapshot tests for hover states + palette selection.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs`

-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","title":"Demo Shortcuts: global key standards + per-screen interactive keybindings","description":"Background
- The demo already supports a help overlay and per-screen keybinding lists.
- Many screens still feel passive; adding meaningful shortcuts is the easiest way to make the demo feel like a 'lab' rather than a gallery.

Goal
- Establish a consistent global shortcut vocabulary and expand per-screen shortcuts so each screen is discoverably interactive.

Global Shortcut Standards (proposed)
- `?` toggle help overlay.
- `Ctrl+K` command palette.
- `Ctrl+P` performance HUD.
- `Ctrl+I` inspector / hit regions overlay (if present).
- `M` toggle mouse mode (see mouse policy task).
- `Esc` closes the topmost overlay (palette/help/modal/tour).

Per-Screen Requirements
- Each screen must have at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts that change state or demonstrate a capability.
- Every shortcut must appear in help overlay under the active screen.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shortcut set is coherent across screens (same key means same kind of action).
- No conflicts with text input screens (respect focus).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for help overlay including global bindings + screen bindings.
- Snapshot tests for help overlay at 80x24 and 120x40 for at least 3 representative screens.","notes":"Claiming: will consolidate global shortcut vocabulary + ensure help overlay stays in sync; start by auditing current global keys and adding regression tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentLantern","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:12.562979642Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","help","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon contributed 13 integration tests for global keyboard shortcuts (d4ecb564): m/F6 mouse capture, Shift+A a11y, F12 debug, Ctrl+P perf HUD, Ctrl+K palette, ? help toggle, Shift+H/L vim navigation, Esc overlay dismiss, shortcut isolation, toggle idempotence.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:41Z"},{"id":848,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 7 more Esc priority and overlay isolation tests (commit 6d0a8e39): esc_does_not_close_help, esc_does_not_close_debug, esc_does_not_close_perf_hud, esc_priority_palette_before_a11y (verifies 2-stage dismiss), esc_with_help_and_a11y_closes_only_a11y, shortcuts_ignored_while_palette_is_open, number_keys_navigate_to_screens. Total shortcut tests: 20. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:35:23Z"},{"id":850,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 5 more shortcut tests (commit 846945d0): tab_cycles_screens_forward, backtab_cycles_screens_backward, ctrl_t_cycles_theme, q_key_is_quit_signal (isolation check), multiple_overlays_coexist (help+debug+perf HUD can be on simultaneously). Total: 25 global shortcut tests. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","title":"Guided Tour v2: add missing best-of moments + mouse/shortcut narration","description":"Background
- Guided tour mode exists and is deterministic, but the storyboard is currently not showcasing some of the most compelling screens and interactions.

Goal
- Expand and refine the guided tour to highlight the highest-signal demo features, with explicit narration of mouse/keyboard interactions.

Scope / Work Items
- Storyboard updates:
  - Ensure tour covers: Inline mode story, determinism lab, time-travel/snapshots, hit testing/hyperlinks, performance HUD/budgeting, and at least one 'big visual' (VFX/Quake).
  - Include at least 2 'interactive moments' where the tour explicitly demonstrates a click/drag/scroll or keypress sequence.
- Overlay copy updates:
  - Callouts should point out the exact interaction ('Click the tab', 'Scroll here', 'Press Ctrl+K').
  - Keep copy tight and high-signal.

Constraints
- Tour remains deterministic under fixed seed and time-freeze.
- Tour must not depend on real wall-clock delays.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tour completes in ~2-3 minutes and feels like a cinematic product tour.
- Tour demonstrates mouse and shortcuts explicitly.
- Tour does not break inline scrollback story.

Tests / Validation
- Update existing tour E2E harness to cover new steps and assert stable step ordering/log output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.512011698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.439885192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.203082627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.511911070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.979851049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.383701981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.677897754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.893529658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.286884579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.592 -{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","title":"Testing: mouse/shortcuts regression harness (unit + snapshot + PTY)","description":"Background
- Adding mouse routing and lots of shortcuts risks subtle regressions (overlay priority, focus bugs, scrollback theft, nondeterminism).

Goal
- Add a focused, deterministic test suite that locks in the new interaction behavior.

Scope / Work Items
- Unit tests (fast):
  - Mouse dispatch priority (palette > help > tour > chrome > screen).
  - Click activation edge consistency (down vs up).
  - Hover coalescing and drag lifecycle.
- Snapshot tests:
  - At least one snapshot per interaction category (palette hover, list click selection, scrollbar drag).
- PTY / E2E tests:
  - Send real SGR mouse sequences through PTY to validate parsing + routing.
  - Inline-mode run asserts scrollback-preserving defaults (mouse capture disabled unless toggled).

Acceptance Criteria
- Interaction behavior is deterministic and regression-protected.
- Tests are stable across sizes (80x24 and 120x40).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderHollow","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:53:08.823285496Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","e2e","mouse","shortcuts","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.746047285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.036084728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.333059545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.635703478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927366547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Tour screens","description":"Purpose
- Track and drive the mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for the demo's Tour-category experiences.

Included Screens (current)
- Guided Tour (base exists; v2 work tracked elsewhere in bd-iuvb.17.6)
- Dashboard
- Inline Mode Story (scrollback-first constraints)

Interaction Goals (Tour)
- Mouse-first navigation and 'clickable tiles' where the UI suggests it.
- Clear, cinematic affordances: hover highlight, click to drill in, wheel scroll where content overflows.
- Shortcuts that demonstrate capabilities quickly (toggle overlays, switch tiles, trigger scripted sequences).

Definition of Done
- Each included screen has at least 1 meaningful mouse interaction and at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts.
- Help overlay entries are accurate and tested.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"HazyRaven","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:20.734837655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","title":"Dashboard: clickable tiles + mouse routing + power shortcuts","description":"Background
- Dashboard is the demo's cinematic overview. It already lays out multiple 'tiles' that visually read as clickable panes.
- The dashboard view currently registers pane hit regions for several tiles (see `dashboard.rs` registering `chrome::register_pane_hit`), but global mouse routing does not yet use those hits.

Goal
- Make the dashboard feel like a real clickable home screen.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights the tile under the pointer.
- Click a tile to navigate to the corresponding screen.
- Scroll wheel over a tile that has scrollable content scrolls that tile (or shows a hint that it is not scrollable).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Enter`: activate currently highlighted tile.
- `1-9`: jump to specific tiles in a stable order (documented).
- `R`: refresh/reseed any animated tiles deterministically (if used).

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any dashboard tile navigates deterministically and stops the guided tour if active.
- Help overlay lists dashboard shortcuts.
- Snapshot test covers hover highlight + active tile.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/dashboard.rs` (tile layout + pane hit registration)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (mouse routing)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:13:00.386710034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","title":"Inline Mode Story: mouse-friendly without scrollback regression","description":"Background
- Inline Mode is a core differentiator: preserve native terminal scrollback while keeping chrome stable.
- Mouse capture can easily break this by stealing the scroll wheel; this screen must be the 'golden example' of doing it right.

Goal
- Make the Inline Mode story screen interactive and mouse-friendly *while keeping scrollback preservation obvious and intact*.

Mouse Interactions
- Default (mouse mode OFF): wheel scrolls terminal scrollback; screen should explicitly tell the user that this is intentional.
- Clickable elements inside the inline UI:
  - Click on highlighted callouts to jump to the next/previous story beat.
  - Click on 'Try it' prompts to trigger a deterministic mini-action (e.g., show/hide chrome, toggle a marker line).
- Optional opt-in: allow toggling mouse mode ON for this screen with explicit warning that wheel now scrolls UI.

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Keys to step the story forward/back, toggle example modes, and toggle mouse mode.
- Include a one-key 'reset story' action that restores the initial state deterministically.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline scrollback is preserved by default (wheel scrolls terminal scrollback).
- Any opt-in mouse mode change is explicit and discoverable.
- Screen is still demonstrably interactive (click + shortcuts) even in default scrollback-preserving mode.
- Help overlay accurately describes the interaction contract and tradeoffs.

Tests / Validation
- PTY E2E: Inline mode run asserts mouse capture is not enabled by default.
- Snapshot test covers the explanatory copy and the interactive affordances.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/inline_mode_story.rs`
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","mouse","scrollback","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
